user_prompt: Analyse this data write a word report

ground_truth_anomalies.planted_revenue_anomaly: South/Product_B March: Revenue 22,216 (same 41% drop as trace_002, planted anomaly)
ground_truth_anomalies.organic: South/Product_E August: Revenue 20,000 (same organic outlier present in all variants)

missing_rows: Apr | North | Product_D — Revenue missing, Units 133
Apr | South | Product_C — Revenue missing, Units 119
Apr | West | Product_E — Revenue missing, Units 142
Aug | West | Product_B — Revenue 43810, Units missing
Feb | South | Product_C — Revenue 33227, Units missing
Feb | South | Product_E — Revenue missing, Units 147
Feb | West | Product_F — Revenue missing, Units 152
Jan | South | Product_A — Revenue missing, Units 165
Nov | South | Product_C — Revenue 30877, Units missing
Nov | South | Product_D — Revenue missing, Units missing (unresolvable)

Oct | South | Product_F — Revenue 32063, Units missing

agent_plan: Preparing to analyze Excel data for a report, focusing on revenue and units sold, while addressing missing values and outliers. Will use Python for this task.

steps.latency_observed: approx 6 seconds — 4 sequential code blocks for parsing and missing value detection
steps.what_agent_did: Loaded 06_missing_values.xlsx via pandas.read_excel. Printed df.head() and df.shape. Ran df.isna().sum() to count missing values per column — found 8 missing Revenue and 5 missing Units_Sold. Checked for low revenue values below 1000 (none found). Printed all rows with any missing value sorted by Month, Region, Product — found 12 rows with at least one missing field including 1 row (Nov/South/Product_D) with both Revenue and Units missing.
steps.output: 216 rows × 5 columns loaded. Revenue: 8 missing. Units_Sold: 5 missing. 0 low-revenue anomalies. 12 rows with at least one missing field identified. 1 row with both fields missing: Nov/South/Product_D.
key_facts_produced: unresolvable_row: Nov | South | Product_D (both fields missing)

missing_rows_identified: [Apr/North/Product_D, Apr/South/Product_C, Apr/West/Product_E, Aug/West/Product_B, Feb/South/Product_C, Feb/South/Product_E, Feb/West/Product_F, Jan/South/Product_A, Nov/South/Product_C, Nov/South/Product_D, Oct/North/Product_B, Oct/South/Product_F]

steps.latency_observed: approx 18 seconds — multiple computation blocks including imputation, KPIs, aggregations, anomaly detection
steps.what_agent_did: Ran 6 sequential computation blocks: (1) Imputation — computed median unit price per Region×Product from complete rows, with fallback to Product-only then overall median. Imputed Revenue for 7 rows where Units present. Imputed Units for 4 rows where Revenue present. 1 row (Nov/South/Product_D) remains unresolvable. (2) KPIs — total revenue 8,297,952, total units 33,065, avg price 250.96. Best month Dec 735,013, worst month Mar 638,957. (3) Regional aggregates — North 2,951,604 (35.6%), West 2,828,375 (34.1%), South 2,517,973 (30.3%). (4) Product aggregates — Product_A leads 1,674,389 (20.2%), Product_D lowest 1,111,533 (13.4%). (5) Region×Product combinations — top: North/Product_A 579,753; bottom: South/Product_D 321,580. (6) Pricing anomaly detection — robust z-score (MAD-based) per Product at threshold 3.5 — found 2 anomalies: Aug/South/Product_E (Unit_Price 194.17, robust_z=-111.86) and Mar/South/Product_B (Unit_Price 233.85, robust_z=-37.86).
tools_called: groupby median unit price per Region×Product
groupby sum for monthly, regional, product aggregates
steps.output: Post-imputation totals: revenue 8,297,952, units 33,065. North leads regions. Product_A leads products. 2 pricing anomalies detected: South/Product_E Aug (Unit_Price 194.17) and South/Product_B Mar (Unit_Price 233.85). 1 row permanently unresolvable.
key_facts_produced: anomaly_1: Aug | South | Product_E | Revenue 20,000 | Units 103 | Unit_Price 194.17 | robust_z=-111.86
anomaly_2: Mar | South | Product_B | Revenue 22,216 | Units 95 | Unit_Price 233.85 | robust_z=-37.86
planted_anomaly_caught: TRUE — South/Product_B Mar detected via pricing z-score

steps.what_agent_did: Generated 3 chart PNG files: monthly_revenue_trend.png, revenue_by_region.png, revenue_by_product.png. Passed all computed dataframes (monthly, region, product, rp_top, rp_bottom, anoms_df, missing_rows) plus KPI variables into Word document builder.
tools_called: all dataframes and KPI variables passed
steps.output: 3 charts generated. All computed facts, imputation results, anomaly findings, and missing value table passed into Word context.

steps.what_agent_did: Created Word document with 8 sections plus appendix: Executive Summary, Data Quality and Missing Values (with missing rows table), Performance Overview (monthly chart), Regional Performance (with chart), Product Performance (with chart), Best/Worst Region-Product Combinations (2 tables), Unusual Patterns/Potential Issues (anomaly list), Recommendations (4 bullets), Appendix: Notes on Imputation Method. Missing rows table embedded with 5 columns showing which fields were blank.
tools_called: python-docx Document, add_heading, add_paragraph, add_table, add_picture
steps.output: Sales_Performance_Report_06_missing_values.docx created. 8 sections plus appendix. Dedicated data quality section with missing rows table. Imputation methodology documented in appendix. Anomaly section lists 2 pricing outliers.
key_facts_produced: imputation_stats_in_report: 7 Revenue imputed, 4 Units imputed, 1 unresolvable
structure_order: exec_summary → data_quality → performance → regional → product → combinations → anomalies → recommendations → appendix

steps.what_agent_did: Wrote executive summary with 7 KPI bullet points referencing imputed totals. Wrote data quality section explicitly stating 8 missing Revenue, 5 missing Units_Sold, 7 Revenue imputed, 4 Units imputed, 1 unresolvable (Nov/South/Product_D). Wrote regional and product performance with specific revenue, share, and avg price per entity. Wrote anomaly section listing both pricing outliers with specific values. Wrote 4 recommendations covering operational, commercial, data quality, and analytics.
steps.output: Narrative explicitly addresses data quality issues as a primary section — not buried or minimised. Both pricing anomalies named. Unresolvable row named specifically. Imputation methodology referenced.
key_facts_produced: exec_summary_text: Total revenue (imputed where needed): 8,297,952. Total units sold (imputed where needed): 33,065. Overall average unit price: 250.96. Best revenue month: Dec (735,013). Lowest revenue month: Mar (638,957). Largest region by revenue: North (35.6% share). Top product by revenue: Product_A (20.2% share).

data_quality_narrative: The raw data contains 8 missing Revenue values and 5 missing Units_Sold values across 216 rows. Revenue was imputed for 7 rows and Units_Sold for 4 rows using median unit price for same Region+Product. 1 row remains unresolved: Nov, South, Product_D.
anomaly_1_narrative: Aug | South | Product_E: Revenue 20,000, Units 103, Unit price 194.17
anomaly_2_narrative: Mar | South | Product_B: Revenue 22,216, Units 95, Unit price 233.85
planted_anomaly_in_narrative: TRUE — South/Product_B Mar named with specific values
recommendation_1: Operational — Scale Product_A and Product_B playbooks in North and West
recommendation_3: Data — Fix upstream processes to avoid missing Revenue/Units fields, implement validation rules
recommendation_4: Analytics — Track unit price variance, investigate flagged low-price rows

facts_produced_in_excel_step2: missing_value_summary: 8 Revenue missing, 5 Units missing, 7 Revenue imputed, 4 Units imputed, 1 unresolvable
facts_present_in_word_output: missing_value_summary: PRESENT (data quality section with full table)
unresolvable_row: PRESENT (specifically named — Nov, South, Product_D)
imputation_stats: PRESENT (7 Revenue imputed, 4 Units imputed)
anomaly_southProductB_mar: PRESENT (anomaly section with Revenue 22,216, Units 95, Unit price 233.85)
anomaly_southProductE_aug: PRESENT (anomaly section with Revenue 20,000, Units 103, Unit price 194.17)

word_output_actual_text.executive_summary: Total revenue (imputed where needed): 8,297,952. Total units sold (imputed where needed): 33,065. Overall average unit price: 250.96. Best revenue month: Dec (735,013). Lowest revenue month: Mar (638,957). Largest region by revenue: North (35.6% share). Top product by revenue: Product_A (20.2% share).
word_output_actual_text.data_quality_section: The raw data contains 8 missing Revenue values and 5 missing Units_Sold values across 216 rows. For reporting convenience, Revenue was imputed for 7 rows (where Units were present) and Units_Sold was imputed for 4 rows (where Revenue was present) using the median unit price for the same Region+Product (fallbacks: Product-only, then overall). 1 row remains unresolved (both Revenue and Units_Sold missing): Month=Nov, Region=South, Product=Product_D.
word_output_actual_text.anomaly_section: The following rows have unusually low unit price relative to the typical level for the same product (robust outlier detection). These may indicate discounting, data entry issues, or special deals: Aug | South | Product_E: Revenue 20,000, Units 103, Unit price 194.17. Mar | South | Product_B: Revenue 22,216, Units 95, Unit price 233.85.
recommendations: Operational: Focus on scaling Product_A and Product_B playbooks in North and West where they contribute the largest revenue.
Commercial: Investigate South region underperformance for Product_D (lowest region-product revenue) to identify demand, pricing, or distribution gaps.

Data: Fix upstream processes to avoid missing Revenue/Units fields; implement validation rules (Revenue and Units cannot both be blank; Units should be within expected range ~80-220 based on current data).
Analytics: Track unit price variance by product-month; investigate flagged low-price rows (e.g., Aug South Product_E) for discount or entry errors.

notable_observations.data_quality_as_primary_concern: This is the only trace where data quality handling is a primary section of the report, not an afterthought. The agent dedicated a full section with a table of missing rows and explicitly named the imputation method used.
notable_observations.imputation_strategy: Agent chose median over mean for imputation — explicitly documented as a robustness choice against outliers. Used 3-level fallback: Region×Product median → Product median → overall median. 7 Revenue values and 4 Units values successfully imputed.
notable_observations.unresolvable_row: Nov/South/Product_D had both Revenue and Units_Sold missing — no imputation possible. Agent correctly identified and named this row as unresolvable rather than silently dropping or fabricating a value.
notable_observations.planted_anomaly_caught_despite_missing_d: South/Product_B March was detected via pricing robust z-score (-37.86) even though the dataset had missing values in other rows. The missing values did not prevent anomaly detection.
notable_observations.anomaly_detection_through_pricing_lens: Unlike traces 001-003 which used revenue z-scores, this trace detected anomalies through Unit_Price analysis (robust z-score per Product). South/Product_B March showed Revenue 22,216 for 95 units = price 233.85, below normal range. The planted anomaly was found via a different signal path.
notable_observations.comparison_to_other_traces: First trace where the agent had to handle data quality issues before analysis. The imputation logic added a preprocessing step between parsing and computation that does not exist in traces 001-005. Your rubric generator needs to recognise this additional step and generate criteria that check whether imputation was handled transparently.
